steps.title: Identify the pattern across the measures
steps.content: For all three measures, the aware-group ratings exceed the control-group ratings: 5.0 > 3.9, 92.00 > 46.00, 11.4 > 5.4. The decisive clue is this consistent advantage across every measure.

steps.title: Find the choice that states this pattern
steps.content: D directly states the ratings “were higher for all three measures, indicating greater overall well-being for these participants,” which expresses exactly what the data shows. A misreads the numbers as individual variation rather than group means, B claims the opposite direction, and C cites control-group values incorrectly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
